content.text:
{"id":"FRG-246","title":"Objects change sprite when state transitions","description":"## Problem

The stove stays as stove_off.png even when its state is cooking or done. Books stay closed. Easel stays blank. Object state machines work internally but have no visual feedback.

## Available Assets

* stove_off.png / stove_on.png
* book_closed.png / book_open.png
* easel_blank.png / easel_painting.png / easel_finished.png
* cup_empty.png / cup_full.png

## What to do

1. Define a sprite map per object: { stateName: spriteKey }
2. On each state transition (in objectEngine), emit the new sprite key
3. Scene.js swaps the sprite texture on the Phaser game object
4. Map: stove cooking/done->stove_on, idle->stove_off; bookshelf reading->book_open, idle->book_closed; easel painting->easel_painting, done->easel_finished, idle->easel_blank; tea_set brewing/ready->cup_full, idle->cup_empty

## Acceptance Criteria

* At least stove, bookshelf, easel, and tea_set visually change on state transition
* Sprites swap back to default on idle state
* No flickering or missing textures","projectMilestone":{"id":"a6eb53b7-72b4-453e-9e23-aa42a629b7a4","name":"M2: Visual sim (main page)"},"priority":{"value":2,"name":"High"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-246/objects-change-sprite-when-state-transitions","gitBranchName":"devlab/frg-246-objects-change-sprite-when-state-transitions","createdAt":"2026-03-09T04:29:23.533Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-13T08:42:50.921Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Dev in Progress","labels":[],"attachments":[{"id":"97ef363d-a5d5-4b30-99ff-7531ecea55e6","title":"FRG-246: add tea_set object with sprite state transitions","subtitle":null,"url":"https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium/pull/6"}],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Project Manager","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","assignee":"Project Manager","assigneeId":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"AI Aquarium","projectId":"0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"}

{"id":"FRG-246","title":"Objects change sprite when state transitions","description":"## Problem

The stove stays as stove_off.png even when its state is cooking or done. Books stay closed. Easel stays blank. Object state machines work internally but have no visual feedback.

## Available Assets

* stove_off.png / stove_on.png
* book_closed.png / book_open.png
* easel_blank.png / easel_painting.png / easel_finished.png
* cup_empty.png / cup_full.png

## What to do

1. Define a sprite map per object: { stateName: spriteKey }
2. On each state transition (in objectEngine), emit the new sprite key
3. Scene.js swaps the sprite texture on the Phaser game object
4. Map: stove cooking/done->stove_on, idle->stove_off; bookshelf reading->book_open, idle->book_closed; easel painting->easel_painting, done->easel_finished, idle->easel_blank; tea_set brewing/ready->cup_full, idle->cup_empty

## Acceptance Criteria

* At least stove, bookshelf, easel, and tea_set visually change on state transition
* Sprites swap back to default on idle state
* No flickering or missing textures","projectMilestone":{"id":"a6eb53b7-72b4-453e-9e23-aa42a629b7a4","name":"M2: Visual sim (main page)"},"priority":{"value":2,"name":"High"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-246/objects-change-sprite-when-state-transitions","gitBranchName":"devlab/frg-246-objects-change-sprite-when-state-transitions","createdAt":"2026-03-09T04:29:23.533Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-13T08:42:50.921Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Dev in Progress","labels":[],"attachments":[{"id":"97ef363d-a5d5-4b30-99ff-7531ecea55e6","title":"FRG-246: add tea_set object with sprite state transitions","subtitle":null,"url":"https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium/pull/6"}],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Project Manager","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","assignee":"Project Manager","assigneeId":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"AI Aquarium","projectId":"0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"}

content.thinking:
Now I have a good understanding of the codebase. Let me also look at the Engine.js to understand how events flow between the engine and the scene.

The key points from the issue:
1. Objects (stove, bookshelf, easel, tea_set) need to visually change on state transitions
2. Need to define a sprite map per object: { stateName: spriteKey }
3. On each state transition in objectEngine, emit the new sprite key
4. Scene.js swaps the sprite texture on the Phaser game object

From the scene.js, I can see:
- The background is loaded as `'jp'` (Japanese Home image)


- Objects appear to be part of the background rather than individual sprites, and I need to check how they're currently being rendered and whether they have separate sprite instances in the scene
- To implement sprite state transitions, I'll need to load sprite assets for objects that change visually, create individual sprites for them in the scene, map those sprites to object definitions, and then update the sprite texture when state change events fire
- Now I'm looking at how the Engine manages pending events to understand how state transitions get triggered

Now I understand the full architecture. Let me plan the implementation:

1. **In `objects.js`**: 
   - Add a `spriteMap` property to each object def that maps state names to sprite keys
   - Modify `ObjectInstance.interact()` and `ObjectInstance.tick()` to include `spriteKey` in the returned event

2. **In `scene.js`**:
   - Preload the object sprites in `preload()`
   - Create individual object sprites in `create()`, storing them in a map (`this._objectSprites`)
   - In `update()`, when handling `pendingEvents`, check for `spriteKey` and swap the texture
   
Now I'm mapping out the sprite transitions for each object based on their state changes, and I need to figure out where each object is positioned in the scene so I can place the sprites at the right coordinates.

Looking at the tile positions, I can calculate the pixel coordinates by multiplying the tile coordinates by the tile size. The tricky part is that these objects are already painted into the background image, so I'll need to overlay individual sprites on top at the exact positions to show the state changes without duplicating the artwork.

I've got the sprite assets mapped out—different states for the stove, bookshelf, easel, and cup, all 48x48 pixels. Now I'm planning how to load these into the scene and set them up to display the correct state based on what's happening in the game.

Loading sprite assets... Setting up the object sprite definitions with their tile positions and initial states, then creating sprite instances for each object in the scene and storing them by ID so I can update them later when object states change.

Now I'm realizing the core issue: I need to make sure every state transition—whether from `interact()` or `tick()` calls—includes the `spriteKey` in the event data, and then have the scene listen for those events to swap the sprite texture on the Phaser game object. The auto-transition events that currently just emit an emoji also need to include the sprite key so the visual updates properly. I need to make sure all state-transition events get pushed to `pendingEvents`, not just those with emojis, since the scene needs to handle sprite swaps the same way it handles emoji floats. Let me trace through the code paths in Engine.js to see where events are being filtered and ensure sprite key events make it through to the pending queue. Still writing object definitions...

Now I need to verify whether the objects are already drawn as part of the background image, since that'll determine how I position the sprite overlays to match up correctly.

The TILE_SIZE is 48 pixels, so I can calculate the exact positions: stove goes at pixel coordinates 144, 144; bookshelf at 720, 144; easel at 96, 336; and tea_set at 240, 240. I'll set the origin to (0, 0) to align with how the background is positioned.

For layering, I'll put the object sprites at depth 1 so they sit above the background but below the characters at depth 10.

Now I'm realizing the character positioning uses a different origin point—their feet are anchored at the bottom of the sprite, positioned at the tile below where they stand. For the objects, I should place them directly on their tiles with the origin at the top-left corner, which will overlay them cleanly on the background.

When the scene initializes, all objects should be in their default idle states—stove_off, book_closed, easel_blank, cup_empty—matching what's already drawn in the background. I'll only create sprites for objects that have entries in the sprite map.

Before implementing, I should check that existing PR to see what's already been done with the tea_set sprite transitions.

Looking at the issue more carefully, it's already marked as "Dev in Progress" and there's a previous PR attached, but that seems to be from an earlier effort. The current task is to handle all four objects changing sprites when their states transition.

I'll create the feature branch `devlab/frg-246-objects-change-sprite-when-state-transitions` and implement the sprite state transitions for all four objects.

content.text: Now I have a good understanding. Let me update the issue state and create the feature branch.

{"id":"FRG-246","title":"Objects change sprite when state transitions","description":"## Problem

The stove stays as stove_off.png even when its state is cooking or done. Books stay closed. Easel stays blank. Object state machines work internally but have no visual feedback.

## Available Assets

* stove_off.png / stove_on.png
* book_closed.png / book_open.png
* easel_blank.png / easel_painting.png / easel_finished.png
* cup_empty.png / cup_full.png

## What to do

1. Define a sprite map per object: { stateName: spriteKey }
2. On each state transition (in objectEngine), emit the new sprite key
3. Scene.js swaps the sprite texture on the Phaser game object
4. Map: stove cooking/done->stove_on, idle->stove_off; bookshelf reading->book_open, idle->book_closed; easel painting->easel_painting, done->easel_finished, idle->easel_blank; tea_set brewing/ready->cup_full, idle->cup_empty

## Acceptance Criteria

* At least stove, bookshelf, easel, and tea_set visually change on state transition
* Sprites swap back to default on idle state
* No flickering or missing textures","projectMilestone":{"id":"a6eb53b7-72b4-453e-9e23-aa42a629b7a4","name":"M2: Visual sim (main page)"},"priority":{"value":2,"name":"High"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-246/objects-change-sprite-when-state-transitions","gitBranchName":"devlab/frg-246-objects-change-sprite-when-state-transitions","createdAt":"2026-03-09T04:29:23.533Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-13T08:42:50.921Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Dev in Progress","labels":[],"attachments":[{"id":"97ef363d-a5d5-4b30-99ff-7531ecea55e6","title":"FRG-246: add tea_set object with sprite state transitions","subtitle":null,"url":"https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium/pull/6"}],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Project Manager","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","assignee":"Project Manager","assigneeId":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"AI Aquarium","projectId":"0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"}

    10→ *   speech:      'string'                           — shown as character speech bubble
    11→ *   emoji:       '🍳'                               — floating emoji on transition
    12→ */

feat: swap object sprites on state transitions (FRG-246)

- Add spriteMap to stove, tea_set, bookshelf, and easel object defs
  mapping each state name to its sprite key
- ObjectInstance stores spriteMap and includes spriteKey in events
  emitted by both interact() and tick()
- Engine pushes events with emoji OR spriteKey to pendingEvents so
  sprite transitions always reach the scene
- Scene preloads all object state sprites, overlays them on the
  background at tile positions, and swaps textures on each event

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "feat: swap object sprites on state transitions (FRG-246)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Adds `spriteMap` to stove, tea_set, bookshelf, and easel object definitions, mapping each state name to its sprite key
- `ObjectInstance` now emits a `spriteKey` field in events from both `interact()` and `tick()`
- `Engine.js` pushes events with `emoji` **or** `spriteKey` to `pendingEvents` so sprite transitions always reach the scene
- `scene.js` preloads all 9 object state sprites, overlays them on the background at each object's tile position, and swaps textures when a state-transition event arrives

## Sprite maps
| Object | State → Sprite |
|---|---|
| stove | idle→stove_off, cooking/done→stove_on |
| tea_set | idle→cup_empty, brewing/ready→cup_full |
| bookshelf | idle→book_closed, reading→book_open |
| easel | idle→easel_blank, painting→easel_painting, done→easel_finished |

## Test plan
- [ ] Start the sim and observe each object at idle — sprites should match the default background artwork
- [ ] Have a character interact with the stove; confirm it switches to `stove_on` while cooking/done and returns to `stove_off` on idle
- [ ] Repeat for tea_set (cup_empty ↔ cup_full), bookshelf (book_closed ↔ book_open), and easel (blank → painting → finished → blank)
- [ ] Confirm emoji floats still appear correctly alongside sprite changes
- [ ] Confirm no flickering or missing-texture errors in the console

Closes FRG-246

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

content.content: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser/pull/18

content.text:
{"id":"FRG-246","title":"Objects change sprite when state transitions","description":"## Problem

The stove stays as stove_off.png even when its state is cooking or done. Books stay closed. Easel stays blank. Object state machines work internally but have no visual feedback.

## Available Assets

* stove_off.png / stove_on.png
* book_closed.png / book_open.png
* easel_blank.png / easel_painting.png / easel_finished.png
* cup_empty.png / cup_full.png

## What to do

1. Define a sprite map per object: { stateName: spriteKey }
2. On each state transition (in objectEngine), emit the new sprite key
3. Scene.js swaps the sprite texture on the Phaser game object
4. Map: stove cooking/done->stove_on, idle->stove_off; bookshelf reading->book_open, idle->book_closed; easel painting->easel_painting, done->easel_finished, idle->easel_blank; tea_set brewing/ready->cup_full, idle->cup_empty

## Acceptance Criteria

* At least stove, bookshelf, easel, and tea_set visually change on state transition
* Sprites swap back to default on idle state
* No flickering or missing textures","projectMilestone":{"id":"a6eb53b7-72b4-453e-9e23-aa42a629b7a4","name":"M2: Visual sim (main page)"},"priority":{"value":2,"name":"High"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-246/objects-change-sprite-when-state-transitions","gitBranchName":"devlab/frg-246-objects-change-sprite-when-state-transitions","createdAt":"2026-03-09T04:29:23.533Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-13T08:47:02.878Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Test in Progress","labels":[],"attachments":[{"id":"0613136b-22c9-4f09-9b77-d85c3db0de7e","title":"feat: swap object sprites on state transitions (FRG-246)","subtitle":null,"url":"https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser/pull/18"},{"id":"97ef363d-a5d5-4b30-99ff-7531ecea55e6","title":"FRG-246: add tea_set object with sprite state transitions","subtitle":null,"url":"https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium/pull/6"}],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Project Manager","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","assignee":"Project Manager","assigneeId":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"AI Aquarium","projectId":"0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"}

content.text:
## Status
DONE

## What Changed
- `src/objects.js` — Added `spriteMap: { stateName: spriteKey }` to stove, tea_set, bookshelf, and easel defs; stored `spriteMap` on `ObjectInstance`; included `spriteKey` in events returned by `interact()` and `tick()`
- `src/Engine.js` — Changed both event-push conditions to `ev.emoji || ev.spriteKey` so sprite-only transitions (no emoji) still reach the scene
- `src/scene.js` — Preloads 9 object state sprites; overlays a `Phaser.GameObjects.Image` for each mapped object at its tile position; swaps texture via `setTexture(ev.spriteKey)` when a state-transition event arrives

## Key Decisions
- Object sprites are placed at depth 1 (above background, below characters at depth 10) to avoid z-order issues
- Used `setOrigin(0, 0)` to align sprites to the tile top-left, consistent with the background image placement
- Sprite creation in `create()` iterates `OBJECT_DEFS` directly so any future object with a `spriteMap` gets a sprite automatically

## PR / Branch
- Branch: `feature/FRG-246-object-sprite-state-transitions`
- PR: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser/pull/18

result:
## Status
DONE

## What Changed
- `src/objects.js` — Added `spriteMap: { stateName: spriteKey }` to stove, tea_set, bookshelf, and easel defs; stored `spriteMap` on `ObjectInstance`; included `spriteKey` in events returned by `interact()` and `tick()`
- `src/Engine.js` — Changed both event-push conditions to `ev.emoji || ev.spriteKey` so sprite-only transitions (no emoji) still reach the scene
- `src/scene.js` — Preloads 9 object state sprites; overlays a `Phaser.GameObjects.Image` for each mapped object at its tile position; swaps texture via `setTexture(ev.spriteKey)` when a state-transition event arrives

## Key Decisions
- Object sprites are placed at depth 1 (above background, below characters at depth 10) to avoid z-order issues
- Used `setOrigin(0, 0)` to align sprites to the tile top-left, consistent with the background image placement
- Sprite creation in `create()` iterates `OBJECT_DEFS` directly so any future object with a `spriteMap` gets a sprite automatically

## PR / Branch
- Branch: `feature/FRG-246-object-sprite-state-transitions`
- PR: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser/pull/18
